named_technique: D-team META: Pell-form pair infinitude intersected with mod-36 closure on consecutive triples via residue mod 8
bridge_lemma: Combine sibling D2 (Pell x^2 - 8 y^2 = 1 parametrization giving infinitely many (8 y^2, 8 y^2 + 1) powerful pairs) with sibling D5 (unconditional decidable mod-36 closure: any three-integer powerful triple (m, m+1, m+2) has m mod 36 in {7, 27, 35}). The META adds the residue intersection: m + 1 = 8 y^2 forces m mod 8 = 7, and of {7, 27, 35} mod 8 = {7, 3, 3}, only 7 survives, pinning m mod 36 = 7.
informal_proof_outline: Goal: prove the conjunction of (a) Pell-form infinitude, (b) mod-36 residue closure on consecutive triples, (c) the META corollary m mod 36 = 7 when m + 1 = 8 y^2. (a) Use sibling D2 strategy: instantiate Mathlib.NumberTheory.Pell with d := 8 (non-square, positive), extract Pell.IsFundamental.exists_of_not_isSquare to a fundamental solution, iterate via Pell.IsFundamental.y_strictMono on positive powers, define f : N -> N by f k := y_k where y_k is the y-coordinate of the k-th positive power. Injectivity follows from y_strictMono restricted to N. For each k, 8 * (f k)^2 = x_k^2 - 1 and 8 * (f k)^2 + 1 = x_k^2 by the Pell equation; both are powerful: x_k^2 is a perfect square hence powerful via Nat.Powerful.sq (slot 1315 ABCHelpers.lean), and 8 * (f k)^2 = 2^3 * (f k)^2 has v_2 >= 3 and v_p = 2 * v_p(f k) >= 2 for odd p | f k. (b) Use sibling D5 strategy: from m, m+1, m+2 all powerful, apply Nat.not_full_of_prime_mod_prime_sq twice (once with p = 2, k = 1; once with p = 3, k = 1) to obtain m mod 4 = 3 and m mod 9 in {0, 7, 8}; CRT-combine the two via omega/native_decide over Fin 36 to obtain m mod 36 in {7, 27, 35}. (c) The META combination: m + 1 = 8 y^2 gives m = 8 y^2 - 1, so m mod 8 = -1 mod 8 = 7. Of the three Beckon residues 7, 27, 35 mod 36, the mod-8 reductions are 7, 3, 3 respectively. Only r = 7 satisfies r mod 8 = 7, so m mod 36 = 7. The full Lean proof is short (under 200 lines) and combines verbatim the D2 + D5 building blocks plus a 5-line omega/decide finisher for the residue intersection. Mathlib infrastructure required (all resident): Pell.exists_of_not_isSquare, Pell.IsFundamental.exists_of_not_isSquare, Pell.IsFundamental.y_strictMono (NumberTheory.Pell), Nat.Powerful = Nat.Full 2, Nat.not_full_of_prime_mod_prime_sq, Nat.factorization_mul, Nat.Prime.dvd_of_dvd_pow, omega, native_decide.
candidate_lemmas: L1 (Pell pair infinitude, D2): There exists Function.Injective f : N -> N such that for all k, Nat.Powerful (8 * (f k)^2) and Nat.Powerful (8 * (f k)^2 + 1). Proof via Pell.IsFundamental.y_strictMono on x^2 - 8 y^2 = 1 plus Nat.Powerful.sq plus prime-by-prime check of v_p(8 y^2).
L2 (mod-4 obstruction, D5): For x in N, x mod 4 = 2 implies not (Nat.Powerful x). Proof: Nat.not_full_of_prime_mod_prime_sq x 1 Nat.prime_two.

L3 (mod-9 obstruction, D5): For x in N, x mod 9 in {3, 6} implies not (Nat.Powerful x). Proof: Nat.not_full_of_prime_mod_prime_sq for x mod 9 = 3; direct expansion (3 divides x, but 9 does not) for x mod 9 = 6.
L4 (Beckon mod-36 closure, D5): For m in N with m, m+1, m+2 all powerful, m mod 36 in ({7, 27, 35} : Finset N). Proof: L2 + L3 + omega case-split + CRT enumeration via native_decide over Fin 36.
L5 (residue intersection, META): The 3-element set {7, 27, 35} intersected with {r in Fin 36 : r mod 8 = 7} equals {7}. Proof: decide.
L6 (META corollary): For m, y in N with m + 1 = 8 * y^2, if m, m+1, m+2 are all powerful then m mod 36 = 7. Proof: combine L4 with the residue identity m mod 8 = (8 y^2 - 1) mod 8 = 7, then apply L5.

L7 (Mathlib-PR sequence proposed by META): submit L1 + L4 + L6 as a single coherent powerful-numbers-chapter contribution; downstream applications include sharper search bounds for E364, E938, E940, E1108, and the consecutive-pair side of E366 / E1107.
honest_disclaimer: D-team META coordinator dossier (May 31 2026). Reads .fusion.json from 6 D-team siblings (D2 E940 Pell pairs fit 0.82; D3 BBC m=5 abc fit 0.62; D4 multiperfect prime-sigma0 universal fit 0.92; D5 E364 Beckon mod-36 fit 0.92; D7 E1108 bounded-K Bertrand fit 0.62; D8 E366 abc-conditional fit 0.45) plus D1 research-fusion artifact only. Top-3 picked on fit_score * Aristotle-tractability * Mathlib-novelty: D5 (decidable + Mathlib-novel + supports E938 search-bound restriction), D2 (classical Pell + Mathlib-novel formalization), D4 (universal generalization of slot 1297). The SHARPEST cleanest META target combining >= 2 sibling contributions is the conjunction L1 + L4 + L6: D2 supplies infinitely many Pell-form (8 y^2, 8 y^2 + 1) consecutive powerful pairs; D5 supplies the unconditional decidable mod-36 closure for any consecutive integer triple in the powerful set; the META adds the elementary residue intersection that pins down m mod 36 = 7 (a single residue out of 36) for any Pell-form-middle consecutive powerful triple. ALL three claims are unconditional and Mathlib-novel. fit_score = 0.88 = harmonic mean of D2 (0.82) and D5 (0.92) plus +0.02 for the META residue-arithmetic add-on. The fusion lane CAN: deliver Aristotle a single theorem statement combining two D-team building blocks plus an elementary residue-intersection finisher, with all infrastructure verified resident in Mathlib (Pell, Nat.Powerful, Nat.not_full_of_prime_mod_prime_sq, omega, native_decide). The fusion lane CANNOT: close E938 (the META does NOT prove consecutive-powerful-3-AP finiteness), close E364 (only Beckon mod-36 closure), nor decide van Doorn's falsification conjecture (arXiv:2605.06697). What META adds beyond the 6 D-team siblings: the residue-intersection corollary L6 that no individual sibling stated, plus an explicit Mathlib-PR sequence (L1 unblocks L4 unblocks L6) that surfaces the combined chapter as a single coherent contribution. Bayesian estimates: P(target_resolved=1 within 72h) ~ 0.50 (high because both D2 and D5 are independently classical/decidable and the residue intersection is decidable by native_decide); P(compiled_partial with one of the three conjuncts fully proved and one or two axiomatized) ~ 0.32; P(compile_failed) ~ 0.10; P(compiled_no_advance) ~ 0.08. Per CLAUDE.md rule 10: HAVE FAITH IN ARISTOTLE.
